title: stipa × pseudocapillata
intro: Stipa × pseudocapillata, also known as the false capillaria grass, is a hybrid plant species in the grass family (Poaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The medicinal properties of Stipa × pseudocapillata lie in its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, making it a potential natural remedy for various digestive issues. The health benefits of consuming this plant include the improvement of digestion, relief from nausea and vomiting, and a decrease in inflammation in the digestive tract. In traditional herbal medicine, Stipa × pseudocapillata can be prepared as a tea by infusing the dried leaves and flowers in hot water, or as a tincture by mixing the plant extract with a carrier liquid.
